behavioral_changes.summary: The /summary, /trends, and /errors/top actions now expose only startTime/endTime DateTimeOffset query inputs and route date handling through the new strict resolver instead of the old dateRange flow.
When a bound is omitted, strict date resolution fills it independently: startTime defaults to now minus 7 days and endTime defaults to now.
Requests are rejected when startTime is older than the last 60 days.
Requests are rejected when endTime is in the future.
Requests are rejected unless endTime is strictly after startTime, so equal instants and reversed ranges both fail.
Requests are rejected when the requested interval is longer than 60 days.
Same-day timestamp windows are allowed as long as endTime is later than startTime, even though both bounds are later reduced to day strings for SQL.
Summary previous-period calculation now parses and formats ISO date strings with InvariantCulture, removing dependence on ambient culture.
Resolved DateTimeOffset bounds are normalized to UTC yyyy-MM-dd strings before query-service calls, so offsets affect the forwarded day boundary.

grounding_evidence.reason: Introduces the 60-day cap used by the strict start floor and max-window validation.
Summary now binds startTime as a DateTimeOffset query parameter.
Summary now binds endTime as a DateTimeOffset query parameter.
Summary switches to the new strict date-contract resolver.
Previous-period computation now parses the start date with InvariantCulture.
Previous-period computation now parses the end date with InvariantCulture.
Previous-period start date is now formatted culture-invariantly before the query call.
Trends now binds startTime as a DateTimeOffset query parameter.
Trends now binds endTime as a DateTimeOffset query parameter.
Trends switches to the new strict date-contract resolver.
Top-errors now binds startTime as a DateTimeOffset query parameter.
Top-errors now binds endTime as a DateTimeOffset query parameter.
Top-errors switches to the new strict date-contract resolver.
Missing startTime now defaults to now minus the standard 7-day range.
Missing endTime now defaults to the current instant.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: return (null, null, BadRequest(new { error = $"startTime must be within the last {MaxStrictDateRangeDays} days." }));
grounding_evidence.reason: Adds the explicit 400 guard for startTime older than the 60-day floor.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: return (null, null, BadRequest(new { error = "endTime cannot be in the future." }));
grounding_evidence.reason: Adds the explicit 400 guard for future endTime values.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: return (null, null, BadRequest(new { error = "endTime must be after startTime." }));
grounding_evidence.reason: Adds the strict ordering guard that rejects equal or reversed ranges.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: return (null, null, BadRequest(new { error = $"Date range must not exceed {MaxStrictDateRangeDays} days." }));
grounding_evidence.reason: Adds the explicit 400 guard for windows longer than 60 days.
Resolved startTime is converted to a UTC date string before being sent to SQL/query-service layers.
Resolved endTime is converted to a UTC date string before being sent to SQL/query-service layers.

scenario_sketches.title: Insights v2.0.6 endpoints expose startTime/endTime only
scenario_sketches.rationale: Reflect over GetSummaryAsync, GetTrendsAsync, and GetTopErrorsAsync to assert the public query contract contains DateTimeOffset start/end parameters and no longer depends on the legacy dateRange input.

scenario_sketches.title: Omitted bounds default independently to last-7-days-through-now
scenario_sketches.rationale: Exercise a small matrix of calls with neither bound, start only, and end only; assert the forwarded dates use now-7d when start is missing and now when end is missing, rather than deriving one side from the other.

scenario_sketches.title: Reject startTime older than the 60-day floor
scenario_sketches.rationale: Probe just inside the floor, exactly at the floor, and just beyond it to confirm only older-than-60-days requests fail with 400.

scenario_sketches.title: Reject future endTime values
scenario_sketches.rationale: Validate endTime just before now, exactly now, and just after now to pin the future-time guard.

scenario_sketches.title: Require endTime to be strictly after startTime
scenario_sketches.rationale: Cover reversed ranges, equal instants, and a minimally greater endTime to prove equality is invalid while a same-day later timestamp is valid.

scenario_sketches.title: Reject ranges longer than 60 days
scenario_sketches.rationale: Check spans just under 60 days, exactly 60 days, and slightly over 60 days to verify only over-cap windows are rejected.

scenario_sketches.title: Accept same-day timestamp windows
scenario_sketches.rationale: Send startTime and endTime on the same UTC day with start<end, expect success, and verify both forwarded query dates collapse to the same yyyy-MM-dd bucket.

scenario_sketches.title: Summary previous-period calculation is culture-invariant
scenario_sketches.rationale: Run GetSummaryAsync under a non-default CurrentCulture and assert the previous-period start date passed to the query service is still derived correctly from the ISO day strings.

scenario_sketches.title: Offset-bearing inputs normalize to UTC day strings
scenario_sketches.rationale: Use DateTimeOffset inputs whose local date differs from their UTC date and assert the query-service start/end strings follow the UTC date component, not the caller-local one.
